title: Performance, Capacity, Efficiency, and Cost Tradeoffs · Decision Retrieval
summary: A delayed review reconstructs performance, capacity, efficiency, and cost tradeoffs from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for capacity and cost evidence model after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Set task, response, interaction, payload, memory, CPU, queue, accessibility, and energy budgets tied to user conditions.
Model actors, operations, arrival patterns, concurrency, data sizes, locality, think time, bursts, failures, and growth.
Measure end-to-end and component behavior with controlled environments, percentiles, resource signals, variance, and profiler evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Performance Budget · changed case · Review Performance Budget
steps.instruction:
review case — A fast average hides slow devices, large payloads, p99 queue growth, inaccessible loading states, dependency quotas, and an unbounded bill. The review variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Which action demonstrates Performance Budget without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Performance Budget · changed case · Review Performance Budget.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for set task, response, interaction, payload, memory, cpu, queue, accessibility, and energy budgets tied to user conditions. For Performance Budget · changed case · Review Performance Budget, keep Performance Budget connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A fast average hides slow devices, large payloads, p99 queue growth, inaccessible loading states, dependency quotas, and an unbounded bill. The review variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Performance Budget condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: ACM IEEE AAAI Computer Science Curricula 2023 — Controls competency-based progression, project work, professional practice, ethics, accessibility, security, testing, and the integration of knowledge with observable performance.

stimulus.title: Unit Economics case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A fast average hides slow devices, large payloads, p99 queue growth, inaccessible loading states, dependency quotas, and an unbounded bill. The review variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Attribute fixed and variable build, run, storage, transfer, support, accessibility, and failure costs to useful product outcomes.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Cheaper infrastructure automatically creates a more efficient product.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Attribute fixed and variable build, run, storage, transfer, support, accessibility, and failure costs to useful product outcomes. Artifact under review: capacity and cost evidence model.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Unit Economics, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Unit Economics record says capacity and cost evidence model looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Unit Economics.

For separate evidence from confidence, apply Unit Economics without its stated preconditions, omit the capacity and cost evidence model trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Unit Economics for capacity and cost evidence model.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Attribute fixed and variable build, run, storage, transfer, support, accessibility, and failure costs to useful product outcomes.
